methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $18M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Dendra Systems is a Series A-stage climate and energy company founded by Susan Graham and Irina Fedorenko, having raised $18M to date. Valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 3 private peers in the same sector and stage (Graphyte at $200M, Fourth Power at $150M, and Steady Energy at $110M), supplemented by reference to a broader set of 8 private companies and 1 public comparable.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $18M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A to next-stage progression, implies a valuation of $36M. Peer-median analysis across the 3 same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $150M, with a range spanning $110M to $200M. The divergence between these approaches reflects material uncertainty in whether Dendra's risk profile and market position align with the higher-funded peer set.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $31M (low) to $172M (high), with a median of $93M. The wide range reflects two competing signals: stage-progression suggests conservative positioning near the lower bound, while peer-median data supports substantially higher valuations. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the peer cohort (whether Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy are true comparables in traction or technology maturity) and whether Dendra's capital efficiency relative to peers justifies a discount or premium to median.
